Empires & Puzzles Costumed Wang Yuanji Review: The Noblewoman Gets an Upgrade
Wang Yuanji's costume trades raw healing for defense and a crucial class change. Is the 'Gentle Calligrapher' the upgrade we've been waiting for?
Costumed Wang Yuanji: From Glass Cannon Healer to Iron Fortress
Costumed Wang Yuanji: The Gentle Calligrapher Rewrites the Meta

The "Artisan" Costume for Wang Yuanji transforms the fragile overhealer into a sturdy support anchor. Released in late January 2025, this costume attempts to modernize one of the most beloved 4-star healers. By swapping her class to
, the developers have addressed her biggest weaknesses: vulnerability to mana control and squishiness.
form. Is she better than the original? In almost every PvP scenario, the answer is yes.

The "Gentle Calligrapher" adopts a more structured, scholarly look. The flowing white robes are replaced with detailed yellow and gold attire, emphasizing her role within the Kingdom. The artwork feels more grounded and "combat-ready" in a support sense—she looks less like a deity floating above the battlefield and more like a tactician shielding her allies. It’s a solid visual update that matches her new defensive capabilities.
The costume provides a much-needed stat modernization:
. These are respectable numbers for a 4-star hero in 2026. She is no longer the "glass cannon" healer she once was. She can take a hit, which is essential for a Slow hero who needs time to charge.
The costume tweaks the formula of the original skill in three key ways:
Remains the same. Still the best counter to Fiends in the 4-star bracket.
(down from 625). This is a significant drop—roughly 17% less healing.
This trade is generally worth it in PvP. 625 HP is great, but if your team has low defense, that extra HP vanishes in one hit. A +30% Defense buff makes
point of HP (including the boosted ones) worth more effective health. It effectively turns her into a "Mini-Vivica."
As a Cleric, she can resist mana reduction (Manashield). This effectively increases her speed against mana controllers like Proteus or Li Xiu, as she won't be set back.
The +30% Defense buff is substantial. On a hero with 1000 Defense, that's +300 Defense.

Still very high, but noticeably less "bursty" than the base form.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The costume brings a new passive that is absolutely game-changing for minion wars.
Standard Kingdom bonus: Mana and Healing for minions. Still great, still encourages pairing her with summoners.
If you pair her with a minion summoner (like

), their minions become almost permanent fixtures. They can't be wiped by Grimble or Goretooth. This ensures your Kingdom Family bonus (Mana/Heal per minion) stays active, fixing her slow speed issue permanently.
A solid defensive Aether power. At the start of battle, she regenerates health, helping her survive the initial onslaught of fast snipers before she can fire her first heal.
The base hero was legendary for her heal. The costume arrives in an era where "just healing" isn't enough. Heroes need to do multiple things.
The stat bump brings her back into relevance. 774 Defense (base) is workable. The defense buff makes her relevant in a meta dominated by heavy hitters.

This costume makes Wang Yuanji a complete package. She is safer to use than the base version. If you have the costume, level it first for the stat bonus, then use the costume side for difficult map stages where enemies hit hard.
is the reason to use this costume. If you run a 3-Kingdoms stack in war, she is mandatory to protect your minion mana engine from counters like Grimble. Don't overlook this utility.
Community reaction to the costume was mixed but generally positive about the utility.
"And another costume that makes an already not especially good hero worse. Thanks for that,"
grumbled one pessimist in the polls.
However, savvy players noted the class change:
The shift to defense buffer was debated:
"Is 30% defense worth losing 100 HP heal? In this sniper meta, yes."

Costumed Wang Yuanji matures the hero from a one-trick pony (huge heals) into a versatile support unit. While she may not have the raw "wow" factor of the original's massive HP bar boost, she is a smarter, safer, and more consistent hero to bring into battle.
A successful modernization. She trades excess for endurance, and in the long war of Empires & Puzzles, endurance wins.
